notes: Two cleanly separated clusters. Q3.5-4B captures all four hard discriminators flagged in the ground truth (function-name apostrophe, trailing underscore on validatePlaylistKeys_, no apostrophe in aujourdhui.json, 500 as the third arg to rateLimitedForEach outside the arrow fn) but systematically misreads the escaped name string as 'aujour'hui' (dropping the d). Q3VL-8B-Q8 captures the escaped name string correctly but misses every other hard discriminator (adds an apostrophe to the function name, drops the trailing underscore, inserts an apostrophe into aujourdhui.json breaking its escaping, and misplaces/strands the 500 — rep1-rep4 omit the closing paren for rateLimitedForEach entirely, which is syntax-breaking). Both models consistently fabricate a catch block that is absent from the truncated screenshot (over-completion). rep0 of Q3VL is the weakest of its cluster because it additionally places 500 inside Combiner.push rather than rateLimitedForEach. Ground truth was treated as authoritative on the trailing underscore and backslash escaping, both of which are visually subtle and that two OCR passes tended to drop.
